items.html: This is the meaning of "and comprehended the dust of the earth in a Shalish [third-measure]": the measure of the earth, which is Malchut, was one third of the Yesod, which is the measure of the Atara [crown]; and she was then called Atara. By this you will understand the matter of the several names and appellations that Malchut has — and all of them are true, for she has many roots, according to the changing of the times. And by this it will be clarified to you how it is said in The Zohar that Malchut is the discernment of the Atara in the Yesod, while she is also the Partzuf of the female in her own right, his spouse. The matter rests on one premise, namely: in anything of Kedusha [holiness], its root is never uprooted from its place, even though it afterwards departs from there. Hence Malchut was at first the discernment of the Atara in the Yesod of the male, and afterwards she grew more and more, little by little, until after several times she returned to be Panim be Panim [face-to-face] with him. Yet the aforementioned roots were not uprooted because of it.
